description: Auto-scan & Watcher Guardrails for `seshat serve` — fix the memory leak (measured 11.4 GB in 3.5 min, observed 91.8 GB peak) caused by `notify-debouncer-full` recursively walking dangerous cwds. P0: gate watcher launch on successful auto-scan. P1: refuse to auto-scan from denylisted cwds (e.g. $HOME, ~/Library, drive roots) when not inside a git repo.

userStories.title: Add is_dangerous_cwd() helper with per-OS denylist
userStories.description: As a backend module, I need a single function that returns true if a given path is at or under a known-dangerous location, so that `serve` can refuse to scan/watch there.
acceptanceCriteria: New module crates/seshat-cli/src/dangerous_path.rs with `pub fn is_dangerous_cwd(path: &Path, additional: &[String]) -> bool`
Module wired into crates/seshat-cli/src/lib.rs

Per-OS denylist via cfg(target_os): macOS includes $HOME, ~/Library, ~/Documents, ~/Downloads, ~/Desktop, ~/Pictures, ~/Movies, ~/Music, ~/Public, ~/.config, ~/.cache, /, /Users, /Applications, /System, /Library, /private, /tmp, /var, /usr, /etc, /opt

Linux denylist: $HOME, /, /home, /etc, /var, /tmp, /usr, /opt, /root, /proc, /sys, /dev, $XDG_CONFIG_HOME (fallback ~/.config), $XDG_CACHE_HOME (fallback ~/.cache), $XDG_DATA_HOME (fallback ~/.local/share)

Windows denylist: drive roots (regex ^[A-Za-z]:\\?$), %USERPROFILE%, %USERPROFILE%\Documents, %USERPROFILE%\Downloads, %USERPROFILE%\Desktop, %APPDATA%, %LOCALAPPDATA%, %TEMP%, C:\Windows, C:\Program Files, C:\Program Files (x86), C:\ProgramData
Path matching: cwd is dangerous if cwd == entry OR cwd is descendant of entry (use Path::starts_with for component-wise matching, so /var does not match /var2)
Paths canonicalized via std::fs::canonicalize before comparison; symlinks resolved
macOS/Windows comparison is case-insensitive (lowercase via to_string_lossy().to_lowercase()); Linux is byte-exact
$HOME / %USERPROFILE% resolved via dirs::home_dir() (add `dirs` to seshat-cli deps if not present)
Denylist entries that don't resolve on the current machine are silently skipped
Malformed `additional` entries (relative paths) are skipped with a warn log
Unit tests cover: exact match, subdir match, sibling not matched (/var/foo vs /var2), symlink-to-dangerous, case difference on macOS/Windows, missing env-var fallback, malformed additional entry
Tests inject home-dir override via test-only helper; no dependency on real $HOME
Typecheck passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented in crates/seshat-cli/src/dangerous_path.rs with per-OS denylists (macOS / Linux / Windows), component-wise Path::starts_with matching, case-insensitive comparison on macOS/Windows, byte-exact on Linux, dirs::home_dir() resolution, silent skip for unresolvable entries, warn-log for relative additional entries. 17 unit tests cover exact/subdir/sibling/case/symlink/missing-env/malformed-additional. Module wired into lib.rs.

userStories.title: Add configurable additional_denylist_paths to ScanConfig
userStories.description: As a user with non-standard setups, I want to add custom dangerous paths via seshat.toml so that the guardrail is extensible without code changes.
acceptanceCriteria: ScanConfig in crates/seshat-core/src/config.rs gains `pub additional_denylist_paths: Vec<String>` with #[serde(default)] and a doc comment explaining: each entry matches as cwd == path OR cwd is descendant of path; absolute paths only, tilde and env vars NOT expanded
Default impl returns empty Vec; old TOML files without the field continue to deserialize
seshat.example.toml documents the field with a commented example: `# additional_denylist_paths = ["/mnt/nfs", "/Volumes/BackupDrive"]`
Existing ScanConfig round-trip serialization tests still pass
New test verifies field deserializes from TOML and missing field defaults to empty Vec
Typecheck passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Added pub additional_denylist_paths: Vec<String> to ScanConfig in crates/seshat-core/src/config.rs with #[serde(default)] and a doc comment explaining match semantics (cwd == path OR descendant), absolute-only, no tilde/env expansion. Default impl returns empty Vec. Added two new TOML deserialization unit tests (field present and missing). Documented the field in seshat.example.toml. Added toml as a dev-dependency to seshat-core. Existing AppConfig roundtrip test in seshat-cli still passes.

userStories.title: P1 — Block AutoScan when cwd is dangerous and not in git repo
userStories.description: As `serve`, I must refuse to start auto-scan when invoked from a dangerous directory without a git repository, but proceed with a stderr warning when the user explicitly passed --repo.
acceptanceCriteria: In crates/seshat-cli/src/db.rs::resolve_serve_db_or_project_root, before existing branch logic: when explicit_repo.is_none(), check is_dangerous_cwd(&cwd, &scan_config.additional_denylist_paths) && find_git_root(&cwd).is_none(); if true, return Err(CliError::DangerousCwd { path, hint })
When explicit_repo.is_some(), run the same check on the resolved project_root; if true, log a multi-line stderr warn line and continue with normal AutoScan / ExistingDb resolution
New CliError::DangerousCwd { path: PathBuf, hint: String } variant added to the existing CliError location (locate during impl — likely crates/seshat-cli/src/error.rs)

Display impl for DangerousCwd produces multi-line user-friendly message including the offending path, an explanation of denylist behavior, and three concrete suggestions: cd into a real project, run `seshat scan <path>`, or pass `--repo <path>`
Stderr warn line for --repo override case is multi-line, includes ⚠️ prefix, the offending path, and notes that this proceeds because --repo was passed explicitly
Refusal exit code is non-zero (use existing CliError-to-exit-code convention)
On refusal: no DB file created, no directory walking happens

Unit tests cover: cwd inside temp $HOME-like dir with no .git → returns DangerousCwd; cwd inside real git repo (.git subdir) → returns AutoScan or ExistingDb as today; cwd inside dangerous + explicit_repo=Some(safe) → proceeds with no warn; cwd anywhere + explicit_repo=Some(dangerous) → proceeds with stderr warn captured
Tests use tempfile::TempDir and inject home-dir override via test-only helper; no dependency on real $HOME
Typecheck passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Wired the dangerous-cwd guardrail into `seshat serve`. Added `CliError::DangerousCwd { path, hint }` with multi-line Display (offending path + denylist explanation + 3 suggestions). Decomposed `resolve_serve_db_or_project_root` into 4 pure helpers (`build_dangerous_cwd_hint`, `build_repo_override_warning`, `check_serve_dangerous_cwd`, `check_repo_override_dangerous`) so all decisions are unit-testable without capturing stderr or mutating cwd/home. Refusal gate fires when `explicit_repo.is_none() && is_dangerous_cwd(cwd) && find_git_root(cwd).is_none()`. Override warn fires when `explicit_repo.is_some() && is_dangerous_cwd(project_root) && find_git_root(project_root).is_none()` — the symmetric condition makes 'safe' = 'has a git root upstream', which is OS-portable (the strict 'safe path' semantic is unreachable on macOS because `/` is in the builtin denylist). Signature `resolve_serve_db_or_project_root(explicit_repo, additional_denylist_paths: &[String])` extended; serve.rs:449 passes `&config.scan.additional_denylist_paths` (US-002 plumbing). 7 new unit tests, 619 total passing in seshat-cli, workspace clippy clean.

userStories.title: P0 — Watcher does not start when auto-scan failed
userStories.description: As `serve`, when scan_state reports a failure (e.g. project too large), I must not spawn the watcher tokio task, regardless of config.watcher.enabled.
acceptanceCriteria: In crates/seshat-cli/src/serve.rs around line 752, change gating from `if watcher_enabled` to `let watcher_should_start = watcher_enabled && scan_state.error_message().is_none(); if watcher_should_start { ... }`
Extract gating into a free function `fn watcher_should_start(enabled: bool, state: &ScanState) -> bool` for testability
Inside the spawned watcher task, after wait_scan.wait_for_scan(), re-check wait_scan.error_message() (race guard: scan may have failed during the wait)
If error_message is Some after wait_for_scan: log info!, bail out without calling start_watcher, send Err(WatcherError::ScanFailed(msg)) on watcher_tx
New WatcherError::ScanFailed(String) variant added to crates/seshat-watcher/src/error.rs with Display and Debug impls
Confirm existing startup-banner branch at serve.rs:921-922 still triggers watcher_status = "disabled (auto-scan failed)"
Optionally enrich banner with failure reason from scan_state.error_message(), e.g. "disabled (auto-scan failed: too many files)"
No notify-debouncer-full::Debouncer is constructed when scan_state.error_message().is_some()

Unit test exercising the gate: build a ScanState via ScanState::in_progress() then mark_failed("test"), call watcher_should_start, assert false; assert no WatcherHandle is constructed in the relevant path
Typecheck passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Closed the P0 leak class: watcher will not spawn when auto-scan failed, and the spawned task re-checks scan state after wait_for_scan() to catch the race where scan fails during the wait. Added `WatcherError::ScanFailed(String)` variant in `crates/seshat-watcher/src/error.rs` with Display+Debug tests. Extracted `fn watcher_should_start(enabled: bool, state: &ScanState) -> bool` in `crates/seshat-cli/src/serve.rs` (free fn, top of module, with 6 unit tests covering disabled/enabled × not_needed/in_progress/complete/failed). Outer gate at line 754 now reads `if watcher_should_start(watcher_enabled, &scan_state)`. Inside the spawned watcher task, after `wait_scan.wait_for_scan()`, we re-check `wait_scan.error_message()` — if Some, log info!, send `Err(WatcherError::ScanFailed(msg))` on `watcher_tx`, return without calling `start_watcher()`. Banner enriched: `disabled (auto-scan failed: <reason>)` via `Cow<'_, str>` pattern. workspace clippy clean, 625 seshat-cli + 33 seshat-watcher tests passing, 2248 workspace tests passing.

userStories.title: Add integration tests for end-to-end serve guardrails
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want subprocess-level tests that cover the user-visible behaviour of `seshat serve` from various directories so that regressions are caught in CI.
acceptanceCriteria: New file crates/seshat-cli/tests/serve_guardrails.rs
Test 1: `seshat serve` with cwd=temp_dir_under_$HOME (no git) exits non-zero in <1 s, stderr contains 'Refusing to auto-scan'

Test 2: `seshat serve` with cwd=temp_dir/git-init/ (real git repo created via Command::new("git").args(["init"])) starts (poll stderr for 'MCP server' startup line, then send SIGTERM and assert clean exit)
Test 3: `seshat serve --repo /tmp/empty-no-git` starts with stderr containing '⚠️  Serving from a dangerous location' warn line

Test 4: P0 path — start `seshat serve` in a small real project but with auto_scan_limit = 1 in temp seshat.toml to force scan failure; assert stderr banner shows 'watcher: disabled (auto-scan failed)' and process memory (via ps) stays under 200 MB after 30 s
Tests use assert_cmd::Command (add as dev-dep if missing) and tempfile::TempDir
Binary invoked via env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_seshat")
Each test runs in <30 s
Tests gated to skip on Windows where they need POSIX-specific paths (use #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))] where appropriate)
Typecheck passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
Tests pass on macOS and Linux
userStories.notes:
Added crates/seshat-bin/tests/serve_guardrails.rs (4 integration tests, gated #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]). Test 1: dangerous cwd (HOME=tempdir) without git → fast non-zero exit, stderr contains 'refusing to auto-scan'. Test 2: real `git init` repo → MCP startup banner ('Waiting for MCP client connection') appears, refusal does NOT fire. Test 3: positional `seshat serve <dangerous-tempdir>` → stderr contains '⚠️  Serving from a dangerous location' override warn before normal startup. Test 4 (P0): tempdir with `git init` + `seshat.toml` (auto_scan_limit=1) + 2 .rs files → banner shows 'Watcher: disabled (auto-scan failed: Project too large for auto-scan (2 files). Run: seshat scan --verbose)' AND ps RSS stays under 200 MB after a 3 s grace period. Tests use a `StderrCapture` background thread (poll-without-blocking) so tests 2-4 can wait for specific stderr lines and then `child.kill()` for cleanup. Each test pre-creates `<tempdir>/Library/Application Support/seshat/repos/` (or `.local/share/seshat/repos/` on Linux) because Database::open does not create parent dirs; only `seshat scan` does.

Two deviations from the literal PRD path documented as required improvements: (1) test file lives at `crates/seshat-bin/tests/`, not `crates/seshat-cli/tests/` — Cargo only auto-rebuilds the seshat binary when tests live in the same package as the [[bin]], otherwise `cargo test -p seshat-cli --test serve_guardrails` runs against a stale binary; the assert_cmd/predicates dev-deps already exist in seshat-bin so no Cargo.toml changes were needed. (2) The 4th test waits 3 s (not 30 s) before sampling RSS, to keep the test under the per-test 30 s budget; the original 91.8 GB leak class allocated GBs in seconds, so 3 s is sufficient to detect a 200 MB regression while the watcher is gated off.

Also fixed a US-004 banner-display bug exposed by this integration test: the watcher_status match arm `Some(msg) if has_auto_scan` could never fire because the AutoScan failure path sets `auto_scan_project_root = None` (i.e. flips `has_auto_scan` to false at the exact moment we need the failure banner). Removed the `if has_auto_scan` guard so any `error_message().is_some()` produces 'disabled (auto-scan failed: <msg>)'. Safe because `error_message()` only ever becomes Some on the AutoScan failure path. Watcher correctly never spawned (US-004 outer gate already handled that); only the banner display was wrong.

Quality checks: cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings (workspace) ✓; cargo test --workspace --lib → 2248 passing (no regression); cargo test -p seshat-bin --test serve_guardrails → 4 passed in 4.0 s. The 2 pre-existing failures in seshat-bin/tests/scan_integration.rs (V11__branches_table missing — caused by stale ~/Library DB from a newer version) are unrelated and were noted in US-003.

userStories.title: Update CHANGELOG, docs, and bump version
userStories.description: As a downstream user, I need clear release notes and updated docs so I understand the new dangerous-cwd guard and watcher-gating behaviour.
acceptanceCriteria: CHANGELOG.md (create if not present) gets an entry under next-version `### Fixed` describing both fixes: dangerous-cwd refusal with --repo opt-out, and watcher gating on auto-scan failure; entry mentions the 90+ GB leak class being closed
seshat.example.toml documents additional_denylist_paths with a commented example
README.md section about `seshat serve` (if present) updated to mention the dangerous-cwd guard and the --repo opt-out
Workspace Cargo.toml: bump patch version (e.g. 0.1.x → 0.1.(x+1))
All workspace crates re-resolve with the new version (cargo check succeeds)
Typecheck passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Created CHANGELOG.md (Keep a Changelog format, semver-aligned) with a [0.1.1] - 2026-05-06 entry that captures both fixes: (1) the dangerous-cwd refusal in `seshat serve` with the explicit-repo opt-out warn, and (2) the watcher gating on auto-scan failure — both attributed to closing the 91.8 GB peak memory leak class caused by notify-debouncer-full's recursive walk on init. The entry documents the per-OS denylist (macOS / Linux / Windows), the matching semantics (component-wise Path::starts_with, case-insensitive on macOS/Windows, byte-exact on Linux, symlinks resolved). Added section also calls out the new `scan.additional_denylist_paths` config field with TOML example, the new `WatcherError::ScanFailed(String)` variant, the new `CliError::DangerousCwd` variant, and the subprocess integration tests in `crates/seshat-bin/tests/serve_guardrails.rs`. Bumped workspace version from 0.1.0 to 0.1.1 in `Cargo.toml [workspace.package]`; all 10 member crates use `version.workspace = true` so the bump applies uniformly. README.md does not exist at repo root (PRD criterion qualified with 'if present'); no docs/ user-facing serve docs to update either (only research markdown). seshat.example.toml documentation for additional_denylist_paths was already added in US-002 — verified still present. Quality checks: `cargo check --workspace` clean (2.4s incremental), `cargo clippy --all-targets --workspace -- -D warnings` clean, `cargo test --workspace --lib` → 2248 passed / 0 failed across 9 crates (no regression), `cargo test -p seshat-bin --test serve_guardrails` → 4 passed.
